publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Saturday June 13, 2020 at 03:08

did.unittitle: Joaquin Miller Collection

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Acquired from various sources. For more information see catalog cards filed in the collection.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Joaquin Miller Collection is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Joaquin Miller Collection. Yale Collection of American Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections as they are acquired, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.

This collection received a basic level of processing in 2014, including rehousing and minimal organization. This finding aid was produced from a previously existing card set in the Manuscripts Catalog. All pertinent bibliographical information has been retained.
This collection includes materials previously identified by the following call number: Za Miller, Joaquin.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into three series: I. Correspondence, 1871-1931. II. Writings, 1870-1905. III. Other Papers, 1872-1901.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

did.unittitle: Correspondence

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series I is arranged alphabetically by recipient.

did.unittitle: Alger, Abbie, 2 autograph letters, signed, Washington
"Babes in the woods," 1 autograph letter, signed, to "my dear babes in the woods," The Hights, California
Bliss, ---, 1 autograph letter signed, to "My dear Bliss," London
Carmany, John H., 5 autograph letters, signed

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Removed from Songs of the Sunlands , 1873.

did.unittitle: Century , editors, 1 autograph letter, signed, The Hights, Dimond, California
Conway, ---, 1 autograph letter, signed, Boston, Massachusetts
The Critic , editors, 1 autograph letter, signed, San Francisco, with manuscript corrections and directions to the printer by the editors of The Critic
File, Franklin, 1 autograph letter, signed, Oakland, California
Hall, Gertrude, 1 autograph letter, signed, Oakland, California
The Independent , 1 autograph letter, signed, London
Johns, ---, 1 autograph letter, signed, Dimond, California
Kervy, H.P., 1 autograph letter, signed, Oakland, California, with addressed, stamped envelope
Leland, Abigail, 1 autograph letter, signed, New Orleans
Life , editor, 1 autograph letter, signed, to the editor of Life , Washington, D.C.
Mcall, ---, 1 autograph letter, signed, New York
Miller, Dewitt, 1 autograph letter, signed, Oakland, California
Niles, ---, 2 autograph letters, signed, New York, New York
The Overland Monthly , editor, 2 autograph letters, signed, New York City and London
Roberts Brothers, 1 autograph letter, signed, Oakland, California
Roosevelt, Theodore,1 autograph letter, signed, Washington, D.C.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes 1 autograph letter, signed, to "my dear boy," one of Theodore Roosevelt's sons, April 2, 1903, the Hights, Dimond, California.

did.unittitle: Routledge, George, & Sons, 1 autograph note, signed, Oakland, California

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: On a notecard and with printed biographical sketch, corrected, enclosed.

did.unittitle: St. Nicholas , editor, 1 autograph letter, signed, to the editor, New York

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Submitting a story "Capt. Duck," of which a brief summary in an unidentified hand appears on the verso of the letter.

did.unittitle: Stedman, Edmund Clarence, autograph inscription written on reverse of a photograph
Stedman, Laura Woodworth, 1 autograph letter, signed, Oakland, California

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes miscellaneous material connected with Joaquin Miller including newspaper clippings, postal cards, pictures, pamphlets. From the estate of Stedman.

did.unittitle: Stedman, Laura Woodworth, from Juanita Miller, 26 autograph letters, signed, and autograph notes, signed, 9 autograph notes signed on miscellaneous cards

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes photographs, picture-post cards, clippings from magazines and newspapers about Juanita Miller, annotated.

did.unittitle: Stone and Kimball, 3 autograph letters, signed, from Miller, Oakland, California

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Concerning the publication of his The Building of the City Beautiful .

did.unittitle: Woodward, Fred E., 1 autograph letter, signed, from Miller, Oakland, California, with original addressed envelope

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 autograph letter signed from Miller to Fred E. Woodward, December 7, 1911, Oakland, California. With original addressed envelope.

did.unittitle: Unidentified correspondents
1 autograph note, signed, from Miller to unidentified recipient
1 autograph note signed from Miller to "My Dear B"
1 autograph letter, signed, from Miller to unidentified correspondent (Carmany?), New York.
1 autograph letter, signed, from Miller to "My very dear fellow Senlire(?)," Fruitvale, California.
1 autograph note, signed, from Miller to "My dear Miss H--"
1 autograph letter, signed, from Miller to unnamed correspondent, Dimond, California
1 autograph letter, signed, from Miller to "My dear P.(?)," Washington
1 autograph letter, signed, from Miller to "S.," The Hights, California

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: On stationary bearing his printed account of "The Arbor day cross" at top.

did.unittitle: Writings
did.abstract: Series II is organized into two subseries: Poetry and Other Writings.

did.unittitle: Poetry
"Alone," autograph manuscript, signed, Boston, one 7-line stanza
"By the Sun-down Seas," page proof of pages 1-2 and 9-10, with notes by Miller, "A Picture," proof, 27 lines, with note, signed
"The Lost Regiments (A Legend of the Late War)," original manuscript, 7 stanzas
"New York City," corrected proof, with manuscript title and signature

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A poem of 3 ten-line stanzas, used without title, as epigraph to part 2, "On Fifth Avenue," of his The Baroness of New York (Published G.W. Carlton & Co., 1877).

did.unittitle: "A Pioneer of Idaho," printer's copy of original manuscript in hand of Ina Donna Coolbrith, with corrections by Bret Harte

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Published in The Overland Monthly , September 1870, v.5, p.280-286. Published under the title "Rough Times in Idaho."

did.unittitle: "Rome," manuscript, signed, Chicago, Illinois, one 5-line stanza, with positive photostat
"A Sioux Lodge," original manuscript, 10 stanzas, signed
"With Walker in Nicaragua," autograph manuscript of prefatory verses beginning "Come to my Sunland," 8 lines, signed
"Woman," autograph manuscript, signed, one 8-line stanza
Unidentified verse, autograph manuscript, signed, Oakland, one 4-line stanza beginning with the line "Why, I would rather stoop and bear..."
Other Writings
The Building of the City Beautiful , autograph manuscript, in the hand of an emanuensis, with 20 pages in the author's autograph

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Published: Albert Brandt, Trenton, New Jersey, 1905.

did.unittitle: Unidentified manuscripts in Miller's handwriting, with his signature
Miscellaneous folio pages and scraps of typewritten and autograph manuscript and notes, 47 leaves, with 2 leaves bearing clipped printed poems
Other Papers
Autograph inscription
Autograph inscription, Oakland, California, on printed stationery headed: Joaquin Miller Lecture Season, 1900-1901, on verso: photographs of Joaquin Miller at different ages
Autograph inscription signed, Boston
"Isles of the Amazons," manuscript contract for the publication of poem in The Overland Monthly , signed by Miller, San Francisco
Photographs

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains portrait photographs of Joaquin Miller and snapshots of Joaquin and Juanita Miller. Candid photographs include Joaquin Miller hiking in Alaska (1898) and a snapshot his home in Canyon City, Oregon. A series of snapshots show Juanita Miller creating potpourri sachets.

did.unittitle: Portrait photograph of Joaquin Miller by James E. Purdy, Boston
